# codex-acp v0.27.0

> **Unpublished candidate.** The `v0.27.0` tag did not produce a GitHub
> Release because its unsupported Windows build failed inside the pinned
> upstream `codex-utils-pty` dependency. No `v0.27.0` release assets were
> published; the compatibility work is released through Linux-only `v0.27.1`.

`v0.27.0` consolidates the post-`0.26.2` work into one compatibility and safety
release. The adapter now follows the official stable Codex `0.144.6` config and
app-server baseline, accepts new reasoning levels without a closed local enum,
reports enough version identity to diagnose startup skew, and no longer writes
whole editor buffers back after Codex has already changed files on disk.

## Highlights

- Updated the coordinated Codex dependency set to the official
  `openai/codex` `rust-v0.144.6` baseline.
- Added forward-compatible reasoning effort handling, including `max`, `ultra`,
  and future non-empty values.
- Replaced terse config-load failures with read-only startup diagnostics that
  identify the adapter, compiled Codex schema, backend binary, and config path.
- Removed adapter-side full-buffer writeback, eliminating the stale-buffer race
  that could overwrite or duplicate edits in Zed.
- Tightened old-session recovery and removed redundant adapter-owned history
  commands in favor of Zed's native ACP session flow.
- Expanded the lower `Status` surface with configurable limit summaries and
  more useful session, MCP, skill, plugin, and compaction details.

## Codex 0.144.6 compatibility

- All direct Codex crates now come from the same official `openai/codex`
  revision: `rust-v0.144.6`, commit
  `5d1fbf26c43abc65a203928b2e31561cb039e06d`.
- The repository toolchain is pinned to Rust `1.95.0` for this baseline.
- Reasoning effort is now treated as an open, non-empty string contract. Known
  values are `none`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh`, `max`, and
  `ultra`. A future value is retained and round-tripped exactly instead of
  failing adapter parsing or being silently mapped to a lower level.
- Existing backend-provided reasoning effort is preserved even when the model
  catalog has not yet described that value. Catalog defaults are used only
  when the session has no current effort or when the user explicitly changes
  to a model that does not support the selected effort.
- Schema acceptance does not guarantee model support. A selected model or
  backend may still reject an effort that it does not advertise; that failure
  now occurs at the real backend boundary instead of preventing the adapter
  from starting.
- Model discovery requests the complete backend catalog with
  `includeHidden: true`. Hidden models remain excluded from the selector by
  default, can be opted into by explicit model id in
  `selector-preferences.json`, and remain visible when already active in a
  restored session.

## Actionable startup diagnostics

Config-load and `-c` override failures now produce a structured diagnostic
before ACP startup. The report includes:

- a stable failure classification, including a separate
  `unsupported-config-schema-value` classification for likely version skew;
- the `codex-acp` package version;
- the compiled Codex schema release and exact `openai/codex` revision;
- the configured backend command, its resolved executable path, and a
  best-effort `codex --version` result;
- the resolved `CODEX_HOME` and exact `config.toml` path;
- the original parser error without replacing it with a generic message.

The version probe is bounded and best-effort, so an unavailable backend does
not hide the underlying config error. Diagnostics are also strictly read-only:
the adapter does not rewrite a manual `config.toml` or silently fall back to
defaults when the file fails validation.

## File synchronization safety

- Removed all adapter-side full-buffer writeback after Codex file edits.
  `FileChange` and final `Turn diff` items still render normal ACP transcript
  cards, while the backend's filesystem edit remains the source of truth.
- Zed's native file watcher now owns editor reconciliation. Clean buffers
  reload from disk; dirty buffers retain unsaved text and become conflicts for
  the user to resolve.
- Removed the reverse-diff guard and its read-before-write path. ACP exposes no
  expected revision, compare-and-swap token, dirty-buffer query, or conditional
  write method, so another read could only narrow the stale-buffer race rather
  than close it.
- Rename and delete events can no longer be followed by a stale whole-buffer
  write that recreates an old path.
- The same change avoids duplicate snippets, glued values such as
  `10px10px`, and whole-buffer format-on-save churn after `apply_patch`.

This behavior was checked separately on Zed `1.10.2` with closed, clean,
dirty, multi-file, rapid repeated edit, no-final-newline, rename, and delete
fixtures. Clean buffers converged to the latest disk content; dirty text stayed
unsaved and conflicted instead of being overwritten.

## Session and history behavior

- Removed the redundant adapter-owned `/threads` and `/resume` commands from
  both the surfaced command list and parser. Zed's native history continues to
  use ACP `load_session` and `resume_session`.
- A stale Zed history row that has no materialized Codex rollout can now open a
  fresh backend thread under the same ACP session instead of failing the entire
  restore path.
- Other restore failures are still reported. The fallback is intentionally
  limited to the missing-rollout case and does not hide arbitrary backend,
  authentication, or protocol errors.

## Status and integration polish

- The lower selector remains the familiar `Status` control. Its configurable
  default groups include limit summaries, `Session`, `MCP`, `Skills`,
  `Plugins`, and `Compact now`.
- Limit summary variants are owned by `display-maps.json`, so labels and
  thresholds can be changed without adding another runtime hardcode.
- Existing `selector-preferences.json` files can still narrow or reorder the
  visible groups. Generated defaults materialize all supported groups without
  requiring users to reset hand-edited preferences.
- Session details now include compact adapter/backend version, workspace,
  account identity, and plan metadata.
- Skill summaries are loaded through the app-server `skills/list` contract.
- Plugin summaries now retain marketplace load failures and distinguish local,
  Git, npm, and remote source details where the backend provides them.
- Collaboration summaries recognize the backend's `Interrupted` state instead
  of collapsing it into an unrelated terminal status.

## Protocol maintenance

- Updated request, response, tool, image, and path handling for the coordinated
  `0.144.6` app-server protocol shapes while preserving the existing ACP cards
  and session flow.
- Newly encountered app-server requests that the adapter cannot implement,
  including attestation and current-time requests, are explicitly rejected.
  They no longer risk remaining unanswered and stalling the backend.
- Refreshed ACP, Zed, Codex, legacy codex-acp, and official successor reference
  snapshots. The audit confirmed that current ACP, Zed, and Codex app-server
  still expose no atomic versioned editor-write contract.

## Install and upgrade

Download the release installer and pin the requested version:

```bash
curl -fsSLO https://github.com/Qweasd123tg/zed-codex-ACP-CAS/releases/latest/download/install.sh
bash install.sh --download 0.27.0
```

The installer downloads and verifies the matching checksum, smoke-tests the
binary before activation, installs it atomically to
`~/.local/bin/codex-acp`, writes `~/.local/bin/codex-acp.build-info.txt`, and
creates the adapter config home when needed.

After upgrading, restart the external-agent process or the Zed session so Zed
launches the new binary. If Zed's GUI environment cannot resolve the backend
`codex` executable from `PATH`, set `CODEX_ACP_CODEX_BIN` to its absolute path
in the custom agent environment.

## Verification

The release worktree passed the following checks:

```text
cargo test
  266 passed

cargo fmt --all -- --check
  passed

cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
  passed

cargo test --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
  266 passed

git diff --check
  passed

bash script/build_local_release.sh
  passed

./install.sh
  passed
```

The locally cached `.build/codex-acp-current` and installed
`~/.local/bin/codex-acp` binaries matched. Their local verification SHA-256 was
`61c931332729becfb4f6b525329aad7bc5c2ef632f09e18f1370f0d73dec9566`.
Published release artifacts carry their own workflow-generated `.sha256`
files; the local hash above is not a substitute for checking the downloaded
artifact's matching checksum.

## Platform notes

- Linux x86_64 GNU was the only locally tested target in this candidate.
- The candidate workflow attempted Linux, macOS Apple Silicon, and Windows x64.
  Linux and macOS compiled, but Windows failed before the publish job.
- Because the matrix was required, GitHub skipped `Create Release`; none of the
  candidate artifacts became release assets.
- Starting with `v0.27.1`, official prebuilt releases are Linux x86_64 GNU only.
  macOS and Windows are deferred self-build platforms without release support.

## Known limitations and next cycle

- The dirty-buffer matrix above was exercised on Zed `1.10.2`, but this release
  does not claim a complete live Zed UX pass for every changed session,
  selector, tool, and integration path.
- Forward-compatible effort parsing prevents startup breakage, but an effort
  unsupported by the active model can still fail when a turn starts.
- External ACP agents still use Zed's generic grouped selector. Native Thinking
  Mode placement, iconography, and editor-specific controls are Zed-side work,
  not something the adapter can add through the current ACP contract.
- File reconciliation depends on Zed's native watcher. Dirty buffers become
  explicit conflicts; the adapter does not attempt an automatic three-way
  merge. ACP still has no revision or compare-and-swap write primitive.
- Several newer Codex app-server surfaces are not yet exposed through this
  adapter: auto-review flows, richer reset-credit/account usage, child-thread
  and import flows, plugin management, hooks/goals, remote authentication, and
  some newer server requests. Unsupported request variants are rejected
  explicitly rather than partially simulated.
- MCP SSE passthrough and `DynamicToolCall` remain unsupported by the current
  adapter/ACP bridge.
- Generated-image saved-file and native-link presentation remains partial.
- macOS and Windows still need platform-specific maintainers, fixes, and
  runtime coverage before official binary releases return.
